title: Looking for a Job After 45
subtitle: 45岁后再求职：经验为何有时反而成了门槛

paragraphs.en: Ageism and long-term unemployment are most damaging for those in the second half of their careers. Changing careers is hard. Getting back into work after you've been laid off is even harder. Those over 45 make up the bulk of the long-term unemployed, in America and elsewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 年龄歧视和长期失业对职业生涯后半段的人伤害最为深重。转换职业本已困难，被裁员后重返职场则更加艰难。在美国及其他地区，45岁以上的人占长期失业群体的大多数。

paragraphs.en: Hiring managers admit they are reluctant to hire those over 40 or 45, arguing they probably won't be a good "fit," their experience won't be relevant to the workplace, and they'll be unable or unwilling to learn new skills. But the one thing that can really make a difference? More education and training.
paragraphs.zh: 招聘经理坦承，他们不愿招聘40岁或45岁以上的求职者，理由是这些人可能"不合适"，其经验与当前职场需求脱节，且无法或不愿学习新技能。然而，真正能够扭转这一局面的，是什么？答案是：更多的教育与培训。

paragraphs.en: These are the results from a new study by Generation, a nonprofit founded by former McKinsey consultants. They've just published a global survey on midcareer employment, based on surveys of over 5,000 workers and managers in seven countries.
paragraphs.zh: 这些结论来自Generation的一项新研究。Generation是一家由前麦肯锡咨询师创立的非营利组织，他们刚刚发布了一项关于职业中期就业状况的全球调查，数据来源于对七个国家逾5000名工人和管理者的问卷调查。

paragraphs.en: "Older midcareer workers make up the bulk of the long-term unemployed in many countries," Generation's analysts write in their report. Those over 45 comprise over 40% of the long-term unemployed, for example. And if you're out of work past the age of 45, there's nearly a two in three chance you'll be out of work for over a year, they find.
paragraphs.zh: Generation的分析师在报告中写道："在许多国家，年龄较大的职业中期工人构成了长期失业群体的主体。"以45岁以上的人群为例，他们占长期失业者的40%以上。研究还发现，一旦超过45岁失业，有近三分之二的概率会持续失业超过一年。

paragraphs.en: "Midcareer individuals are finding it harder to get jobs," they write. "People age 45+ face persistent and rising pressure in the global job market. They are unemployed for much longer than the average, and their age is indeed one of the greatest barriers to their finding a job."
paragraphs.zh: 报告写道："职业中期的求职者越来越难找到工作。45岁以上的人在全球就业市场面临持续加剧的压力。他们的失业时间远超平均水平，年龄确实是他们求职路上最大的障碍之一。"

words.meaning: adj. 持续的；顽固存在的

paragraphs.en: A substantial part of this, they report, is "widespread ageism" on the part of hiring managers. Although such managers admit that post-45 hires turn out on average to be just as good as or better than younger workers, they still don't want to hire them. "Hiring managers have a strong perception bias against 45+ job candidates — they believe that members of this age group have poor skills and low adaptability," Generation's analysts report.
paragraphs.zh: 报告指出，造成这一现象的重要原因之一，是招聘经理普遍存在的"年龄歧视"。尽管这些经理承认，45岁以后入职的员工平均表现与年轻员工相当甚至更优，他们仍然不愿意雇用这类求职者。Generation的分析师报告称："招聘经理对45岁以上求职者存在强烈的认知偏见，认为这一年龄段的人技能较差、适应能力弱。"

words.meaning: n. 认知偏见；主观偏差
n. 适应能力；应变能力

paragraphs.en: The survey results are remarkable. Hiring managers are three times as likely to rate job applicants age 35 to 44 as a better "fit" than those over 45. They rate the post-45 job seekers lower on average on all three measures — even experience — than those ages 18 to 34. This is true even though nearly nine managers in 10 also said their post-45 workers were as good as or better than younger employees in the same jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 调查结果令人瞩目。招聘经理认为35至44岁求职者更"适合"的概率，是45岁以上求职者的三倍。他们对45岁以上求职者在所有三项评估指标上的平均评分均低于18至34岁群体，甚至连工作经验这一项也不例外。而这一切的发生，尽管近十分之九的经理同时也表示，他们的45岁以上员工与从事相同工作的年轻员工表现一样好甚至更出色。

paragraphs.en: Their biggest fears about hiring those over 45? Managers said they worry that older hires will be reluctant to try new technologies, and will be unable to learn new skills. Besides, the majority of hiring managers are under 45 — and seem reluctant to hire people older than themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 招聘经理对雇用45岁以上求职者最大的顾虑是什么？他们表示，担心年长员工不愿尝试新技术，也无法学习新技能。此外，大多数招聘经理本身年龄在45岁以下，似乎本能地抗拒雇用比自己年长的人。

paragraphs.en: But here's the good news. Older workers who've actively engaged in more education and training relevant to their jobs turn this pretty much on its head. Three-quarters of hiring managers said they'd be more likely to hire an over-45 who had relevant education, training or credentials. Among those over 45 who'd successfully switched careers, 74% said training helped them get their new job.
paragraphs.zh: 但好消息是，积极参与与工作相关的教育和培训的年长求职者，在很大程度上扭转了这一局面。四分之三的招聘经理表示，他们更倾向于雇用拥有相关教育背景、培训经历或资质证书的45岁以上求职者。在成功转换职业的45岁以上群体中，有74%的人表示培训帮助他们获得了新工作。

paragraphs.en: "Midcareer switchers overwhelmingly say that training enabled them to shift to a new career path," the analysts write. And when they asked over-45 candidates about their attitudes, they found that "those who are excited by training are unemployed for less time, receive more job offers, and are more satisfied by the job offers they receive."
paragraphs.zh: 分析师写道："绝大多数职业中期转换者表示，培训使他们得以转向新的职业轨道。"当研究人员询问45岁以上求职者的态度时，发现"那些对培训抱有热情的人，失业时间更短，收到的工作邀约更多，对所收到的邀约也更为满意。"

paragraphs.en: Hiring managers said training was three times more likely to get them to hire applicants over age 45 than, say, government subsidies. What training are we talking about? Obviously it depends on the job. And, yes, managers are most likely to be impressed by industry qualifications you get in school. These things cost money, and time.
paragraphs.zh: 招聘经理表示，培训对促使他们雇用45岁以上求职者的作用，是政府补贴的三倍。我们说的是什么样的培训？这显然取决于具体工作。是的，经理们最可能对求职者在学校获得的行业资格证书留下深刻印象，而这些需要花费金钱和时间。

paragraphs.en: But these aren't the only things helpful. Those who had successfully changed careers after 45 told Generation that education and training had been a big help — and that included in-person, on-the-job training, "informal learning," and online courses — with or without certification.
paragraphs.zh: 但这些并非唯一有效的途径。那些在45岁后成功转行的人告诉Generation，教育和培训帮助很大，涵盖面对面的在职培训、"非正式学习"以及在线课程，无论是否获得认证。

paragraphs.en: If they think we're too old to learn new skills after we're 45, just learning some new skills may have an effect on its own. There's a downside to this. The people least interested in retraining are more likely to come from historically disadvantaged communities, more likely to have low incomes, and are more likely to have the least schooling after getting their high-school diploma or earlier.
paragraphs.zh: 如果人们认为我们45岁后太老而无法学习新技能，那么仅仅去学一些新技能本身就可能产生改变。然而这里有一个隐忧：对再培训最不感兴趣的人，往往来自历史上处于弱势的社群，收入更低，且高中毕业后所受的正规教育也最为有限。

words.meaning: adj. 处于不利地位的；弱势的

paragraphs.en: On average, post-45 job switchers with higher incomes participate in 2.5 training programs each. Those with lower incomes: 0.9 programs on average. Many years ago, I wrote about a study which found that employers are much more likely to hire a college graduate who has some work experience in the industry than a college graduate who took the "right" degree.
paragraphs.zh: 平均而言，收入较高的45岁以上转职者每人参与2.5个培训项目，而收入较低者平均仅参与0.9个。多年前，我曾写过一项研究，发现雇主更愿意雇用有行业工作经验的大学毕业生，而非仅拥有"对口"学位的毕业生。

paragraphs.en: A bank is more likely to hire a literature graduate who spent their summers working in their local bank than a finance or economics graduate who spent their summers surfing or traveling around Italy. They've learned through long experience. The person who worked in the bank is more likely ready, willing and able to be part of the team and do the job from day 1.
paragraphs.zh: 一家银行更有可能雇用那个暑期在当地银行实习过的文学专业毕业生，而非整个暑假都在冲浪或游历意大利的金融或经济学专业毕业生。经验是在实践中积累的。有过银行工作经历的人，更可能从第一天起就做好准备、愿意并有能力融入团队、完成工作。

paragraphs.en: We can rail against "ageism" all we like. It gets us nowhere. And it's easy to forget that employers, like everyone else, are under time pressure and are just trying to solve problems. Any and all "training," including inexpensive and informal training, is going to help resolve their worries about hiring someone over 45. And it has never been easier or cheaper to gain instruction in new skills, thanks to the internet. Online schools like Udemy and Lynda.com offer sweet deals at sign-up. And courses on YouTube are free.
paragraphs.zh: 我们可以尽情抱怨"年龄歧视"，但这于事无补。我们很容易忘记，雇主和所有人一样面临时间压力，只是想解决眼前的问题。任何形式的"培训"，包括低成本乃至非正式的培训，都有助于消除他们对雇用45岁以上求职者的顾虑。而得益于互联网，学习新技能从未像今天这样便捷、低廉。Udemy、Lynda.com等在线学习平台注册即享优惠，YouTube上的课程更是完全免费。

words.meaning: phr. 愤怒谴责；强烈抱怨

paragraphs.en: Bottom line? Yes, it really can be difficult getting a new job after age 45, let alone changing careers. One of the best things we can do to help ourselves is to go online and learn new, relevant skills. Sadly, the people who most need the help are least likely to take it — which raises a challenge for society as a whole. Not only do we not want to see lives go to waste, but we all benefit if more people are working and fewer are unemployed.
paragraphs.zh: 结论是什么？是的，45岁之后找新工作确实很难，更遑论转换职业。我们能为自己做的最有效的事情之一，就是上网学习新的、与工作相关的技能。令人遗憾的是，最需要帮助的人往往最不愿意接受这种帮助——这给整个社会带来了一项严峻挑战。我们不希望看到任何人的潜力被白白浪费，而且，有更多人就业、更少人失业，对我们所有人都是好事。
